clinics.name: Paul Hom Asian Clinic (PHAC)
clinics.focus: Asian and Pacific Islander community; underserved/underinsured patients; multilingual care
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary and acute care, specialty care, lab testing, prescription consultations; works with VN CARES and HLUB under PHAC.
clinics.hours: Every Saturday, 8:00 AM–12:00 PM; check closure dates before going.
clinics.accessNotes: Call to schedule; leave name, phone, reason for appointment, preferred time/date/language.

clinics.name: Clínica Tepati
clinics.focus: Latinx communities; medically vulnerable and undocumented patients in downtown Sacramento
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary and specialty care; English and Spanish services; helps with prescriptions/referrals.
clinics.accessNotes: Walk-ins accepted based on availability. Call Mon–Fri or Sunday 9:00 AM–4:00 PM to schedule.

clinics.name: Imani Clinic
clinics.focus: African American/Black community and underserved patients in Oak Park
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary care, specialty clinics, outreach; care regardless of insurance, address, immigration status, language, religion, gender identity, or finances.
clinics.accessNotes: Walk-ins accepted until 11:00 AM based on availability.

clinics.name: Shifa Community Clinic
clinics.focus: Multilingual, multicultural uninsured communities; primarily South Asian and Muslim communities
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary care; dental, vaccine, women’s health, diabetic retinopathy, mental health, dermatology, podiatry, and legal clinics listed.
clinics.hours: Sunday, 8:00 AM–12:00 PM; by appointment only
clinics.accessNotes: Appointment only according to current clinic website.

clinics.name: Joan Viteri Memorial Clinic (JVMC)
clinics.focus: Uninsured drug users, sex workers, transgender people, and families; Oak Park community
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary care; harm reduction-focused care; prevention, infectious disease education, HIV testing; reproductive and gender health clinic sessions noted.
clinics.hours: Regular clinic: Saturday, 1:00 PM–4:00 PM; reproductive health: last Saturday monthly, 1:00 PM–4:00 PM; gender health: 1st/3rd Fridays, 6:00 PM–9:00 PM by appointment.
clinics.accessNotes: Regular clinic walk-ins accepted; gender health by appointment only.

clinics.name: Bayanihan Clinic
clinics.focus: Filipino immigrants and diverse underserved/uninsured communities in Greater Sacramento
clinics.servicesNotes: Primary/preventive care, specialty services, outreach, telehealth follow-up appointments; new patients must be in-person.
clinics.hours: Saturday, 8:00 AM–12:00 PM; clinic dates may alternate telehealth/in-person/closed.
clinics.accessNotes: Call to make an appointment; walk-ins not guaranteed. Prioritizes medically uninsured patients.

clinics.name: The Willow Clinic
clinics.focus: People experiencing homelessness or housing instability
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary care and screening; specialized services include gynecology, dental, behavioral health, diabetes support, ophthalmology, and smoking cessation.
clinics.accessNotes: First-come, first-served regardless of insurance or documentation status; call/text for telehealth when available.
clinics.address: First Step Communities, 1400 N A St, Bldg A

clinics.name: RIVER Pediatric Clinic
clinics.focus: Under-resourced pediatric populations in Greater Sacramento
clinics.servicesNotes: Free pediatric primary care exams, developmental assessment, sports physicals, lab referrals, vaccinations, acute illness/injury care, vision screenings/vouchers, referrals, mental health workshops.
clinics.accessNotes: Appointments and limited walk-ins; calling ahead is highly recommended.
clinics.address: WellSpace / Hiram Johnson Community Health Center, 3535 65th Street, Bldg C

clinics.name: VN CARES
clinics.category: UC Davis partner clinic / PHAC sister clinic
clinics.focus: Vietnamese community; cancer awareness and prevention
clinics.servicesNotes: Cancer education and free cancer screenings/enrollment; focused on breast, cervical, and prostate cancer awareness/screening.
clinics.hours: First Sunday of the month; PHAC primary-care page also notes cancer screenings on first Sunday.
clinics.accessNotes: Vietnamese-speaking patients can call VN CARES; Saturday PHAC number also listed for clinic days.

clinics.name: Knights Landing One Health Center
clinics.focus: Rural underserved/agricultural communities around Knights Landing, La Tijera, Robbins, and migrant camps
clinics.servicesNotes: Basic health care, dental/immigration medical exam specialty services, veterinary/basic preventative animal care, environmental health connections.
clinics.hours: Every other Sunday, 8:00 AM–2:00 PM; UC Davis page lists first and third Sundays.
clinics.accessNotes: Call with questions or to make appointments; immigration medical exam clinic uses a waitlist.
clinics.email: ime@knightslandingonehealth.com for immigration medical exam waitlist

clinics.name: Hmong Lifting Underserved Barriers (HLUB)
clinics.category: UC Davis partner clinic / PHAC sister clinic
clinics.focus: Hmong community; culturally and linguistically appropriate care
clinics.servicesNotes: Free clinic; cancer screenings for breast, cervical, colon, and prostate cancers; Hepatitis B screening; diabetes care and more noted by public clinic profile.
clinics.hours: Public hours are mixed: UC Davis legacy page lists monthly clinic 8:00 AM–1:00 PM; PHAC current primary care page lists Saturday 8:00 AM–12:00 PM with HLUB as a sister clinic. Call to confirm.
clinics.accessNotes: For Hmong, Mongolian, or Hindi-speaking patients, PHAC page lists HLUB phone all week.

clinics.name: Gender Health Clinic / Gender Hormone Clinic
clinics.servicesNotes: Gender-affirming hormone therapy, STI testing, PrEP prescriptions; Gender Health Center notes this clinic is not primary care.
clinics.hours: Clinic twice a month by appointment only; UC Davis/JVMC pages list 1st and 3rd Fridays, 6:00 PM–9:00 PM.
clinics.accessNotes: By appointment only; new patients may have a wait time according to Gender Health Center.

clinics.name: Nadezhda Clinic
clinics.focus: Medically uninsured Slavic/Russian-speaking community in Sacramento
clinics.servicesNotes: Free primary care with interpretation and culturally sensitive care.
clinics.hours: One Saturday a month, 8:00 AM–12:00 PM
clinics.accessNotes: Call to schedule; clinic dates vary.
